steps.title: Find the shared input $c$
steps.content: Since $p$ is linear with slope $7$, write $p(x)=7x+b$. Use $p(5)=38$: $$7(5)+b=38,$$ so $b=3$. Then use $p(c)=-11$: $$7c+3=-11,$$ so $c=-2$.

steps.title: Fit the line for $t$
steps.content: Now $t(c)=-8$ means $t(-2)=-8$, and the other point is $t(4)=52$. The slope of $t$ is $$\frac{52-(-8)}{4-(-2)}=\frac{60}{6}=10.$$

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The slope of $y=t(x)$ is $10$, which matches D. By hand, the same slope is $\dfrac{52-(-8)}{4-(-2)}=\dfrac{60}{6}=10$.
